spec_match.rationale: The page now models the WICG origin-migration fields directly: migrate_to, migrate_from, explicit manifest IDs, same-site validation, and web-app-origin-association allow_migration.

interactive.rationale: Editable origins, IDs, behavior, install path, toggles, scenario buttons, generated JSON, and live validation all update through runtime script.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede and feature-index card now describe the same side-by-side manifest and association-file handshake validator.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo includes explicit missing-association, cross-site, and missing-new-id failure scenarios alongside the valid happy path.

references.rationale: A labelled references section links to the correct ChromeStatus entry, WICG manifest incubations origin-migration section, and merged spec-language pull request.

design_system.rationale: The touched concept CSS uses design-system variables, paired button background and color overrides, no inline style attributes, and no static-card pattern.

summary: The manifest explorer is now an interactive, spec-aligned handshake validator rather than a static card.
